author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate TransformationMatrix allocation per glyph via prepend_translation! (#601)

`process_glyph_displacement` was allocating a new `TransformationMatrix`
on every character painted. Adding `prepend_translation!`` allows
in-place mutation

Also applied to `move_text_position` to save an allocation per
text-position move.

A small reduction in object allocations when run on MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 174k -> 153k allocations (~12% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 515K -> 433K allocations (~2% reduction)
* `prince1.pdf`: 33k -> 30K allocations (~15% reduction)

## Before

```
$ ruby -I lib scripts/benchmark_allocations.rb

/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/cairo-unicode.pdf
       cairo-unicode    12.915M memsize (    10.128k retained)
                       174.743k objects (    64.000  retained)
                        50.000  strings (    31.000  retained)
/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/type1-arial.pdf
         type1-arial     4.407M memsize (     1.026M retained)
                        64.342k objects (    13.998k retained)
                        50.000  strings (    50.000  retained)
/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/truetype-arial.pdf
      truetype-arial   282.743k memsize (     0.000  retained)
                         3.227k objects (     0.000  retained)
                        50.000  strings (     0.000  retained)
/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/ascii85_filter.pdf
      ascii85_filter   807.037k memsize (   480.000  retained)
                         9.744k objects (    12.000  retained)
                        50.000  strings (     6.000  retained)
/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/prince1.pdf
             prince1     3.956M memsize (     7.648k retained)
                        33.150k objects (     6.000  retained)
                        50.000  strings (     3.000  retained)
/Users/jh/src/pdf-reader/scripts/../spec/data/pdflatex.pdf
```

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate TransformationMatrix allocation per glyph via prepend_translation!

`process_glyph_displacement` was allocating a new `TransformationMatrix` on every character painted.
Adding `prepend_translation!`` allows in-place mutation

Also applied to `move_text_position` to save an allocation per text-position move.

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate String allocation in Reference#hash via integer arithmetic (#600)

`"#{id}:#{gen}".hash` created a String on every Hash key lookup. Use
`(id << 16) ^ gen` for a collision-resistant integer hash with zero
allocations.

A small reduction in object allocations when run on MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 179k -> 174k allocations (~2% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 530K -> 515K allocations (~2% reduction)
* `prince1.pdf`: 34k -> 33K allocations (~2% reduction)

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate String allocation in Reference#hash via integer arithmetic

"#{id}:#{gen}".hash created a String on every Hash key lookup.
Use (id << 16) ^ gen for a collision-resistant integer hash with zero
allocations.

author: James Healy
message:
Intern operator Token objects to avoid one allocation per content-stream op (#599)

`parse_token` was calling `Token.new(token)` for every operator
recognised in the operators hash. Since PDF content streams repeat a
small fixed set of operators (Tj, BT, ET, cm, Td, …) many times, a
class-level `INTERNED_TOKENS` hash now caches each unique Token after it
is created the first time.

A small reduction in object allocations when run on MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 182k -> 179k allocations (~1% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 540K -> 530K allocations (~1% reduction)
* `prince1.pdf`: 36k -> 34K allocations (~5% reduction)

## Before

```
                        50.000  strings (     1.000  retained)
```

author: James Healy
message:
Intern operator Token objects to avoid one allocation per content-stream op

parse_token was calling Token.new(token) for every operator recognised in the operators hash. Since
PDF content streams repeat a small fixed set of operators (Tj, BT, ET, cm, Td, …) many times, a
class-level INTERNED_TOKENS hash now caches each unique Token after it is created the first time.

author: James Healy
message:
Reduce object allocations in various text extraction related code (#598)

A variety of tweaks to text extraction related code to allocate fewer
objects. The difference is significant, the below benchmarks were run on
MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 305k -> 182k allocations (~40% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 731K -> 540K allocations (~26% reduction)
* `prince1.pdf`: 59k -> 36K allocations (~38% reduction)

author: James Healy
message:
Cache parsed encoding file mappings to avoid repeated file loads

Multiple fonts often share the same encoding (e.g. StandardEncoding). Previously each Encoding
instance would read and parse the same file, allocating hundreds of objects per load. Cache the built
Hash per file path in FILE_MAPPINGS, and just dup the cached result for each instance.

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate splat Array and hex interpolations in Encoding#load_mapping

`_m, single_byte, unicode = *l.match(...)` created 2 Arrays per line,
`(MatchData#to_a + nil splat)` and `"0x#{...}"` interpolated 2 Strings per matching line.
Use `=~` with $1/$2 to eliminate these allocs.

author: James Healy
message:
Avoid Point allocation per TextRun in BoundingRectangleRunsFilter

rect.contains?(run.origin) was calling TextRun#origin, which lazily allocates a Point per run.
Precompute the rect bounds once and compare run.x/run.y directly

author: James Healy
message:
Precompute DEFAULT_MAPPING constant to avoid 256 Array allocs per Encoding

Each Encoding#initialize called default_mapping which created 256 two-element [key,value] arrays to
pass to Hash[tuples]. Replace with a frozen class-level constant built once via each_with_object,
and just dup it in initialize.

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate Range and String allocs per text run in PageLayout#local_string_insert

Range.new(index, end) allocated a Range object and String.new(needle) made an unnecessary copy for
every text run placed during to_s. Using the str[start, length]= slice form avoids both.

author: James Healy
message:
Cache UTF-8 strings per glyph code in Font#to_utf8 for CMap-mapped fonts

For CMap-mapped fonts (Identity-H, etc.) called with Integer glyph codes, to_utf8_via_cmap always
ends with cmap.decode(code).pack("U*") — allocating a new String per call. Since glyph codes repeat
heavily within a page, memoize the result in a per-font Hash to pay the pack cost only once per
unique code.

author: James Healy
message:
Avoid Array allocation on every CMap#decode call via frozen EMPTY_CODEPOINTS

Hash#fetch(key, default) evaluates the default eagerly, so @map.fetch(c, []) was allocating a
throwaway [] on every decode call — even on cache hits. Replacing the default with a frozen constant
eliminates one Array per glyph in any CMap-mapped font.

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate 2 wasted Array allocs per glyph in to_utf8_via_cmap; stop @font_size churn

to_utf8_via_cmap was wrapping cmap.decode (already an Array) in another Array then flattening it — 2
needless allocations per glyph. Replaced with direct .pack and flat_map.

Also stopped invalidating @font_size per glyph: font_size depends only on trm.b/trm.d (scale/rotation),
not trm.e/f (translation) which is all that changes between glyphs.

author: James Healy
message:
Make TextRun#origin lazy to avoid allocating a Point per character

Every TextRun was eagerly allocating a Point for @origin, but the hot path
only accesses x and y directly. Now x/y are stored as plain numerics and
the Point is only created on demand (by bounding-rect filters and tests).

author: James Healy
message:
Add jruby 10.1 and truffelruby 34 to the build matrix (#597)

Specs are green on both, lets keep them that way

author: James Healy
message: Add jruby 10.1 and truffelruby 34 to the build matrix

author: James Healy
message:
Reduce object allocations in PDF::Reader::Parser (#596)

A variety of tweaks to this class to allocate fewer objects. The
difference is modest, mostly depending on how much the PDF uses hex
strings and names with `#xx` format. The below benchmarks were run on
MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 326k -> 305k allocations (~6% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 744K -> 731K allocations (~1.7% reduction)
* `prince1.pdf`: 74k -> 59K allocations (~20% reduction)

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate Array+String allocs in hex_string by reusing pack buffer

prepare_hex_token already accumulates all hex chars as one token, so hex_string's str="".dup + str<<token
loop was an unnecessary copy. Use the token directly.

Also replace [str].pack('H*') with a cached @hex_pack_buffer=[""] array to eliminate 9,624 Array
allocations per cairo-unicode.pdf parse.

author: James Healy
message:
Skip dup+gsub in pdf_name when no hex escape sequences present

The vast majority of PDF names contain no #xx sequences, so dup+gsub was
allocating two strings unnecessarily. Now we only gsub when '#' is present.

author: James Healy
message:
Use match? instead of =~ in parse_token to avoid MatchData allocation

Every numeric token (very common) was triggering a MatchData allocation via =~.
Switching to match?() avoids that object entirely.

We need a shim so the code continues to work on rubies 2.1 -> 2.3, and we don't avoid the allocation
there. That's OK though, compatibility is the goal on those old versions

author: James Healy
message:
Reduce object allocations in PDF::Reader::Buffer (#595)

A variety of tweaks to this class to make it allocate fewer objects. The
difference is significant, the below benchmarks were run on MRI 3.4.9:

* `cairo-unicode.pdf`: 486k -> 326k allocations (~32% reduction)
* `pdflatex.pdf`: 936K -> 744K allocations (~20%)
* `prince1.pdf`: 162k -> 74K allocations (~54%)

author: James Healy
message: Make changes in c2fb182 work on rubies 2.1 -> 2.3

author: James Healy
message:
Inline TOKEN_WHITESPACE bytes in case/when to eliminate splat Array allocs

`when *TOKEN_WHITESPACE` expands the array via splat on every loop iteration,
allocating a new Array each time. Inlining the 6 byte values directly into
the when clause eliminates ~105k Array allocations per cairo-unicode.pdf parse.

author: James Healy
message:
Avoid byte.chr String allocation on normal hex string close in prepare_hex_token

When a hex string closes normally with '>' (0x3E), byte.chr was allocating
a new String object. Branch explicitly on 0x3E to push the frozen '>' literal
instead; the rare non-'>' delimiter path keeps byte.chr.

author: James Healy
message:
Hoist [nil, 0x20, 0x0A] + TOKEN_DELIMITER array to a frozen constant

The '/' case in prepare_regular_token was building a new Array on every
PDF name token to check whether the next byte terminates the name.
NAME_TERMINATOR_BYTES is computed once at class load time instead.

author: James Healy
message:
Eliminate dead tok = "".dup allocs per delimiter token in prepare_regular_token

Restructured the nil (EOF) case to push tok inline, and removed the trailing
@tokens << tok guard. This lets us drop 10 unnecessary tok = "".dup resets
that fired whenever a non-empty token was followed by a delimiter.

author: James Healy
message:
Replace byte.chr with frozen string literals for delimiter tokens in prepare_regular_token

byte.chr allocates a new String per call; frozen string literals in a
frozen_string_literal: true file are interned and reused across calls.

author: James Healy
message:
Avoid "".dup allocation in prepare_regular_token when tok is already empty

When hitting a delimiter with an empty tok accumulator, we were always allocating
a new empty string. Now we only allocate when tok had content and was pushed to
@tokens (and thus can't be reused).
